title: Listening - Part 3: Local Events
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a local event they recently attended. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the aspect of the event that impressed each speaker most. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a local event they recently attended. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the aspect of the event that impressed each speaker most. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about a local event they recently attended.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: Our town held a science festival at the college. I thought it would mostly consist of experts giving talks, which can be interesting but sometimes difficult to follow. [19]What I loved was that visitors weren't simply watching: I could control a small robot, create a bridge from simple materials and test how strong it was.[/19] The activities made the ideas much easier to understand. I stayed far longer than I'd intended and took my younger cousin back the next day.||Speaker 2 Man: I went to the food fair expecting to buy bread and cheese, and there was certainly plenty to taste. However, one area had local farmers giving short presentations. [20]I found out why certain crops grow well here, how weather affects prices and what the labels on food packages actually mean.[/20] Since then I've chosen products more carefully at the supermarket. So, although the samples were excellent, learning something useful was the part that stayed with me.||Speaker 3 Woman: Last month a friend persuaded me to attend an evening play. The acting was good, although I have seen stronger performances elsewhere. [21]What was unforgettable was watching it in the courtyard of an old castle, with stone walls on every side and the sky getting darker above the stage.[/21] Even the brief shower added to the atmosphere. I'd go back for almost any show there simply because the surroundings make it such a special experience.||Speaker 4 Man: I helped my parents take my little brothers to the summer street festival, and I was expecting confusion because thousands of people attend. Surprisingly, we never had any problems. [22]There were clear signs to every activity, volunteers answering questions on each corner, and well-planned queues that moved quickly even at the busiest stalls.[/22] It meant we could relax instead of spending the afternoon trying to find things. Whoever arranged it deserves credit.||Speaker 5 Woman: The park near me showed an old film outdoors one Saturday night. The screen wasn't enormous and the sound occasionally disappeared when a plane flew overhead. [23]Still, I loved seeing parents, children and grandparents sitting together on blankets, sharing snacks and chatting to strangers before the film began.[/23] Nobody seemed in a hurry, and the whole park felt welcoming. It was that mood, rather than the film itself, that made me hope they organise another one.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: Our town held a science festival at the college. I thought it would mostly consist of experts giving talks, which can be interesting but sometimes difficult to follow. What I loved was that visitors weren't simply watching: I could control a small robot, create a bridge from simple materials and test how strong it was. The activities made the ideas much easier to understand. I stayed far longer than I'd intended and took my younger cousin back the next day.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: I went to the food fair expecting to buy bread and cheese, and there was certainly plenty to taste. However, one area had local farmers giving short presentations. I found out why certain crops grow well here, how weather affects prices and what the labels on food packages actually mean. Since then I've chosen products more carefully at the supermarket. So, although the samples were excellent, learning something useful was the part that stayed with me.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: Last month a friend persuaded me to attend an evening play. The acting was good, although I have seen stronger performances elsewhere. What was unforgettable was watching it in the courtyard of an old castle, with stone walls on every side and the sky getting darker above the stage. Even the brief shower added to the atmosphere. I'd go back for almost any show there simply because the surroundings make it such a special experience.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I helped my parents take my little brothers to the summer street festival, and I was expecting confusion because thousands of people attend. Surprisingly, we never had any problems. There were clear signs to every activity, volunteers answering questions on each corner, and well-planned queues that moved quickly even at the busiest stalls. It meant we could relax instead of spending the afternoon trying to find things. Whoever arranged it deserves credit.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: The park near me showed an old film outdoors one Saturday night. The screen wasn't enormous and the sound occasionally disappeared when a plane flew overhead. Still, I loved seeing parents, children and grandparents sitting together on blankets, sharing snacks and chatting to strangers before the film began. Nobody seemed in a hurry, and the whole park felt welcoming. It was that mood, rather than the film itself, that made me hope they organise another one.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker most enjoyed being able to take part in practical scientific activities (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The food fair impressed the speaker because she learned useful information about local produce (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The speaker was most impressed by watching a performance in an old castle courtyard (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker praises the clear signage, volunteers and efficient queues (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker particularly enjoyed the warm, inclusive atmosphere at the outdoor cinema (evidence [23]).
